items.final_wording: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

source.title: Self-harm: assessment, management and preventing recurrence
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: Children, young people and adults who have self-harmed, unless a recommendation is age-specific.
source.setting: Community, emergency department, hospital and mental-health care following self-harm.
source.exclusions: Exact for compassionate psychosocial assessment, emergency-department initial assessment, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for physical and mental care, care-plan status, observation and discharge documentation after self-harm. It does not apply a risk score, predict suicide or generate treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording the person’s needs, concerns, strengths, vulnerabilities, context and clinician-performed psychosocial assessment without using a prediction score.

items.rationale: Supports collaborative psychosocial assessment after self-harm for self-harm history documentation; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.7.12–1.7.20 — emergency-department initial assessment and care

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting injury severity, emotional and mental state, distress, immediate safety, safeguarding, willingness for care, observation and care-plan status in the ED.

items.rationale: Supports initial assessment and care after self-harm for self-harm history documentation; no decision is generated.

items.final_wording: Supports recording safeguarding consideration and clinician-confirmed assessment, plan, communication and follow-up before discharge after self-harm.
